sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.85 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Suppose that every Schmidt subgroup of a finite group$G$is${ }$-subnormal in$G$(see Archive 19.84). Is it true that then there is a normal$$-nilpotent subgroup$N G$such that$G/N$is cyclic? An affirmative answer is known if$=\{\{2\}, \{3 \}, \}$. { A.\,N.\,Skiba} Yes, it is true (X.\,Yi, S.\,F.\,Kamornikov, , 560 (2020), 181--191).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
